title: Enforcer One (dreadnaught)

{{Quote|Cap'n Finhead! Somethin' ''big's'' got us in a ''tractor beam!''|C'borp, after the ''Enforcer One'' catches the ''Marauder Starjacker'' in its tractor beam|<ref name="nomi2">[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]]</ref>}}
The '''''Enforcer One''''' was a modified [[Core Galaxy Systems Dreadnaught]] that served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Stenness Node]]–based [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] in the [[Time/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. [[Captain/Legends|Captained]] by an [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] [[Near-Human]] named [[Norufu]], the ''Enforcer One'' was nine hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long and held a crew of forty-five, including the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay]] [[Grimorg]], one of Bogga's top [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcers]]. The ''Enforcer One'' was heavily armed, boasting heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], sixteen [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], and four [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors.

In [[3999 BBY]], the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]] was assigned to protect an [[Ithullan ore hauler]] that was traveling through the Stenness Node from [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. However, the [[Gruna's ore hauler|ore hauler]] was [[Attack on the Ithullan ore hauler|attacked]] by a [[Finhead Stonebone's pirate gang|gang]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] led by Captain [[Finhead Stonebone]], forcing the ''Enforcer One'' to enter the fray. The dreadnaught used its tractor beams to capture the pirate [[Starship/Legends|ships]] ''[[Marauder Starjacker]]'' and ''[[Stenness Raider]]'', and subsequently brought them to [[Bogga's palace|Bogga's estate]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Vo Dasha]].

==Description==
[[File:Enforcer-One_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Enforcer One'' schematics]]
The ''Enforcer One'' was a modified [[Core Galaxy Systems Dreadnaught]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was nine hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long, and had enough cargo space to hold three thousand tons of material. The [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]], after coming into the possession of the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], had extra [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] added, bringing its number of cannons to sixteen. The ship also wielded heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and four [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam projectors]].<ref name="companion" />

The ''Enforcer One'' was originally designed for [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] occupations, using its [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] to blast through planetary defense armadas and then releasing [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]] to deliver an invasion force. Although Bogga acquired the ''Enforcer One'' in a stripped-down state, the ship still had powerful weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. The ''Enforcer One''{{'s}} main weapon was its heavy turbolaser. The [[Power core/Legends|power core]] and [[Laser/Legends|laser]] [[Actuating module/Legends|actuator]] had an advanced cooling system to allow the weapon extended usage, while the long laser barrel was modified with experimental [[Galven coil|galven circuitry]] to allow tighter focus of the beam and increased range.<ref name="egvv" />

''Enforcer One'' had an interior [[Hangar/Legends|landing bay]] nearly three hundred meters long, and a pair of immense ship loading arms that could be used to pull captured vessels into the bay. ''Enforcer One'' carried six [[light tactical fighter]]s which Bogga replaced frequently. The fighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] seldom received much [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]] training, and the Hutt considered the light tactical fighters expendable. ''Enforcer One'' was crewed by an assortment of [[Species/Legends|multispecies]] Hutt [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcers]], including [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]], [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodrans]], [[Weequay/Legends|Weequays]], and [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]]. They served as boarding [[Troop/Legends|troops]] and crew hands,<ref name="egvv" /> and the ''Enforcer One'' had enough room to keep fifty of them aboard.<ref name="companion" />

==History==
[[File:Great_Bogga_the_Hutt.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Bogga the Hutt, owner of the ''Enforcer One'']]
''Enforcer One'' was originally a part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Republic Military/Legends|military]], but after it was decommissioned the ship ended up in the salvage yards of the<ref name="companion" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Ord Trasi/Legends|Ord Trasi]]. ''Enforcer One'' was later retrieved by [[Stenness Node]]–based crime lord Bogga the Hutt at some point, and the Hutt converted the dreadnaught into a devastating [[Realspace/Legends|space]] combat–ready starship. Bogga had extra laser cannons installed onto ''Enforcer One'' for [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] engagement, and it later became the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of Bogga's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].<ref name="companion" /> As such, the ship was docked at [[Bogga's palace|Bogga's estate]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Vo Dasha]]. Bogga appointed [[Norufu]], an [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] [[Near-Human]], to be ''Enforcer One''{{'s}} [[Captain/Legends|captain]], and the Weequay [[Grimorg]] was made the crewmember in charge of keeping control of the ship's boarding troops.<ref name="egvv" /> ''Enforcer One'' brought Bogga victories he never would have secured otherwise, such as the [[Raid on the Nematiec Gang's asteroid base|successful raid]] on the [[Nematiec Gang]]'s [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] base.<ref name="companion" />

In [[3999 BBY]], the ''Enforcer One'', along with starfighter pilot [[Dreebo]] in one of the ship's light tactical fighters, was assigned to protect an [[Gruna's ore hauler|Ithullan ore hauler]] under the command of Captain [[Gruna]]. Nevertheless, the [[Ithullan ore hauler|ore hauler]] was [[Attack on the Ithullan ore hauler|attacked]] by a [[Finhead Stonebone's pirate gang|gang]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] led by Captain [[Finhead Stonebone]]. In an effort to defend the [[Ore/Legends|ore]] hauler, ''Enforcer One'' dispatched Dreebo to attack the pirate ships, but to no avail. Dreebo's starfighter was destroyed by the pirate ore siphoner ''[[Marauder Starjacker]]'', forcing ''Enforcer One'' to reveal itself. With ease, the dreadnaught captured both the ''Marauder Starjacker'' and its sister ship, the ''[[Stenness Raider]]'', in its tractor beam, bringing the ships on board. Once the pirates were within ''Enforcer One''{{'s}} holding bay, Grimorg and his group of boarding troops surrounded the pirate vessels and took the pirates into their custody. ''Enforcer One'' then went to Vo Dasha, and Grimorg brought the shackled pirates before Bogga himself.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Commanders and crew==
''Enforcer One'' was captained by Norufu, an enslaved Near-Human. Norufu had great confidence in the utter power of ''Enforcer One'', and would often toy with enemies attacking the ship—allowing the attackers to fire the first few shots, only to watch them harmlessly dissipate in the dreadnaught's powerful shields, and using the tractor beam to haul around a captured ship before destroying it with ''Enforcer One''{{'s}} heavy turbolaser. Norufu was also in charge of the down-and-out [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] who managed the control, navigation, and weapons systems. ''Enforcer One'' also counted among its crew Grimorg, a Weequay who was one of Bogga's top enforcers. Grimorg's duty was to keep control of the many Vodrans, Klatooinians, Weequays, and Nikto who served as crew hands and boarding troops. Additionally, ''Enforcer One'' contained a group of starfighter pilots who flew the complement of light tactical fighters.<ref name="egvv" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Enforcer One'' first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Saga of Nomi Sunrider|Tales of the Jedi: The Saga of Nomi Sunrider]]'', the second story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released over late [[1993]] to early [[1994]]. ''Enforcer One'' made its first and only appearance in the arc's [[Tales of the Jedi 4|second issue]]. In this appearance the ship was illustrated by [[David Roach]].<ref name="nomi2" />
